The orders dated 10/7/14 in the conservatorship and 7/30/15 in the trust did not order any interest on fees the conservator or trustee voluntarily deferred. Please provide courtesy copies of the petition and any new papers to the assigned examiner by 5/27/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID FRANK PHILLIPS II
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/29/26 due to delays caused by Court technical (computer) issues. Examiner needs more time to review the fourth amendment. How did the executor determine that Amelia Blanco and Johanna Solkhoff (not Solkoff?) are the successors-in-interest to Joel Solkoff (Solkhoff?)? Has an estate been opened for him? Did he have a trust? [LRSF 14.25 G.] Probate Code Section 16370 pertains to trusts. Probate accountings are cash accountings. [LRSF 14.34 B.] Probate Code Sections 16360-16367 pertain to trusts. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Issues raised by an examiner should be addressed in a supplement or an amendment to a petition (or a completely amended petition) that stands on its own, not responding to "examiner's notes," which just highlights the fact that the petitioner and/or attorney failed to provide sufficient or correct information. It is unclear why the fourth amendment references and summarizes prior amendments.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAURICE ELLIS GOLDMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Granted per written order. Courtesy copies are FILE STAMPED, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] (A proper response to issues posted by an examiner is either a SUPPLEMENT that provides additional information or an AMENDMENT that corrects allegations (or a fully amended petition), not a "response" to issues. [CRC 7.3.] Defective notice is corrected by the filing of the proof of service of the correct, complete Notice of Hearing; there is no need to narrate in a pleading how the defect will be cured. Missing bank statements, too, should just be filed along with the statement required by CRC 7.575(b) if they are computer-generated printouts. There is no need for a separate declaration, when a fiduciary supplements or amends her petition.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YU AN YANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. If the decedent's 50% is community property, the petitioner to explain how this fits with the deed's clear statement that it is the decedent's sole and separate property. Parental consent to this characterization may not be enough. The Court may seek more details about the circumstances under which the title company prepared the 2016 deed using the current vesting. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ESTHER J. PAVLOFF
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/13/2026. Need a verified supplement by petitioner(s) to be filed before 4/29/26: (1) The dates of death for all predeceased relatives, including parents and siblings. LRSF 14.18.C. (2) Detailing all efforts made to locate Ronald Demers, including the address where notice was sent. Confirm whether this is Ronald's current address. (3) Identifying whether Daniel's father is William, David, or Ronald. (4) Identifying whether Paula is related to William, David, or Ronald. (5) Stating whether the presumption under Probate Code section 6124 has been established in this case. If so, provide evidence to overcome the presumption of revocation. Several declarations filed by petitioners, counsel, and Christina do not address these issues. The allegations appear to be conflicting, as it is suggested that the decedent would retain only the original will. LRSF 14.20.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE MATTER OF THE LIVING TRUST OF CESAR ASCARRUNZ TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Orders Regarding Authority Of Trustee And Temporary Restraining Relief
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/02/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) No notice of hearing (DE-120) is on file with the court (Probate Code Section 17203). 2) A petition involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries (CRC 7.902). 3) The instant petition requests determination of whether a lawful successor trustee has assumed authority over the trust, but the trust instrument is not attached to the petition. 4) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A). Please address the above issues and deliver courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 05/16/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT BRUCE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/13/2026 for the following issues to be cleared before 4/29/2026: (1) Petitioner's verified supplement to identify the trustee and the beneficiaries of the sole devisee trust. Petition #8 just lists their relationship to the decedent. Also, need clarification whether Sara H is the child of the decedent's brother, while Kassim K and Jenna B are children of the decedent's sister. (2) Notice of Direction for Remote Appearances and Access (LRSF 14.1.C.6) to be given to the persons who are entitled to. (3) Proposed order must be submitted. LRSF 14.7.A.1).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSEPH O. HOLMES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/02/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) A copy of the current vesting deed must be filed for any real property (LRSF 14.26). 2) The instant petition indicates that the order for final distribution in the Estate of Robson S. Homles distributes the subject property to Dorothy Joan Holmes as personal representative of the Estate of Joseph O. Holmes. Petitioner does not appear to be the personal representative of her predeceased spouse's estate. In a verified supplement, please explain whether petitioner intends to seek appointment as personal representative to marshal this asset. Please provide courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 05/19/2026 to avoid additional continuance.
